question: Prepare the following concept map. Environmental Degradation
answer:
Causes of Environmental Degradation:

Use of chemical fertilizers and pesticides for agriculture
A nuclear reactor will not emit radiation
Vehicle will survive noise pollution
oil spill

Visible Effects:

Soil compaction
Extinction of animals and species of animals.
Drying up of freshwater sources
Pollution of rivers and seas

Remedial Measures:

Use of organic fertilizers for agriculture.
Building new nuclear reactors far and wide from animal habitats.
Increased use of public vehicles and regular inspection of vehicles. Precautions to reduce noise pollution.
Spraying of specific pollution control chemicals with the help of helicopters.
subject: History Political Science Social Science 1
chapter: History and Political Science [English] Standard 9 International Problems

question: Explain India’s position in establising human rights.
answer:
India’s position in establishing human rights:

In the Indian Constitution, human rights have been given the place of Fundamental Rights. The constitution gives the responsibility of the protection of the weaker sections like women and minorities to the government alongside that of the Fundamental Rights.
 In 1993, the Human Rights Protection Act was passed. As per this law, the National Human Rights Commission and the State Human Rights Commissions were established.
The National and State Human Rights Commission were set up in 1993 through the Human Rights Protection Act. It looks after cases of human rights violation.
subject: History Political Science Social Science 1
chapter: History and Political Science [English] Standard 9 International Problems

question: Narrate the effects of terrorism and suggest remedies to control it.
answer:
Effects of terrorism:

It causes the death of many innocent lives as the war zones are usually civilian areas.
External and internal security of a country is at risk because of the threat of attacks.
In areas like Syria, terrorist attacks have caused huge numbers of refugees from the area.

Remedies:

International cooperation of governments is the most important step in counter-terrorism.
Exploitation of any community must be stopped as it’s often the discontent that leads to terrorism and rebellion.
Black marketing, human trafficking and illegal manufacturing must be stopped as it is the source of power for terrorist organizations.
subject: History Political Science Social Science 1
chapter: History and Political Science [English] Standard 9 International Problems

question: Find out about the State Commission for Protection of Child Rights with the help of you teachers.
answer:
The State Commission for Protection of Child Rights has been established under the Protection of Child Rights Act 2005. Its main objective is to protect the rights of children and take measures for their development.
The function of the commission is to investigate cases of violation of child rights, implement laws and schemes for child development, and take special measures for juvenile delinquency and neglected children.
subject: History Political Science Social Science 1
chapter: History and Political Science [English] Standard 9 International Problems

question: Find out about the Great Sparrow Campaign in China and the Chipko Movement in India.
answer:
The Great Sparrow Campaign in China:

Mao Zedong introduced the Great Sparrow Campaign (part of Four Pests Campaign) where people were asked to kill all the sparrows to save grain.
Millions of sparrows died and this led to a tremendous increase in insect population which in turn destroyed the crops.
After the crops were destroyed, millions of people in China died of starvation. It became one of the greatest tragedies of China.

Chipko Movement:

The Chipko movement began in 1970s in the Northern Himalayas as a non-violent movement to save the trees from being cut.
 The cutting of trees was a threat to the forest ecology and livelihood of the villagers. It was led by Sunderlal Bahuguna in 1980s.
The greatest achievement of the movement is the 15-year ban on felling of trees in the Himalayan region by the Uttarakhand government.
subject: History Political Science Social Science 1
chapter: History and Political Science [English] Standard 9 International Problems

question: Write your opinion on whether big dams cause problems.
answer:
Yes, big dams cause problems:

Clearing of land: Building huge dams requires clearance of a large area of land which causes environmental destruction and movement of people from the area.
Breaking the natural flow: Dam construction breaks the natural flow of the river which may affect the migratory patterns of birds and animals and the availability of water downstream.
Rehabilitation of people: This is the most negative effect of building large dams as often the people are not rehabilitated and lose their livelihoods. Building dams around rivers leads to deforestation, which reduces biodiversity. In the natural ecosystem, it creates a disturbance. The construction of dams involves the creation of large reservoirs. Construction of dams requires huge sums of monetary investment.
subject: History Political Science Social Science 1
chapter: History and Political Science [English] Standard 9 International Problems

question: Write a Report of how the Earth Day was observed in your school.
answer:
Celebrating Earth Day
Ridhima Chauhan
Our school celebrated Earth Day through a series of activities on 22nd April. The assembly held a half an hour session on steps that every student can take on an individual level. It included basic habits like switching off electrical appliances when not in need to creative actions like reuse of old clothes, recycling of paper etc. The best part of the day was the Eco-competition where the 2 students from each class were to bring anything made out of reused materials. Students made amazing and creative products like wall hangings, cards, pen stands, bags, paper boxes and even beautiful dresses. The day was marked not only by awareness but also enthusiasm on the part of the students.
subject: History Political Science Social Science 1
chapter: History and Political Science [English] Standard 9 International Problems
